report:
## Profitability

Costco and PepsiCo present a striking contrast in profitability structure: Costco operates on razor-thin margins typical of warehouse retail but has delivered consistent, accelerating profit growth, while PepsiCo commands far higher margin rates on a smaller revenue base but saw profitability retreat in FY2025. Costco grew revenues from $226.95B in FY2022 [COST:Q0] to $275.24B in FY2025 [COST:Q3], with operating income rising from $7.79B [COST:Q8] to $10.38B [COST:Q11] and net income climbing from $5.84B [COST:Q4] to $8.10B [COST:Q7]—implying a net margin of roughly 2.9% in FY2025, consistent with Costco's membership-driven, low-markup model. PepsiCo, by contrast, generated $93.93B in FY2025 revenues [PEP:Q3] against a gross profit of $50.86B [PEP:Q15], reflecting a gross margin above 54%, but operating income fell to $11.50B [PEP:Q11] from a peak of $12.89B in FY2024 [PEP:Q10], and net income declined to $8.24B [PEP:Q7] from $9.58B the prior year [PEP:Q6]. In absolute dollar terms, PepsiCo still earns more net income than Costco on roughly one-third the revenue, underscoring its structurally superior margin profile—but the directional trends favor Costco, whose profits have grown every year in the window while PepsiCo's have reversed.

**COST:** Costco has demonstrated steady and accelerating profitability improvement, with net income rising from $5.84B in FY2022 [COST:Q4] to $8.10B in FY2025 [COST:Q7] and operating income reaching $10.38B [COST:Q11], all while revenues expanded to $275.24B [COST:Q3]. Although absolute margins remain thin relative to consumer staples peers, the consistent upward trajectory in both operating and net income reflects the strength of Costco's membership model and disciplined cost management.

**PEP:** PepsiCo's profitability profile is structurally superior in margin terms—gross profit of $50.86B [PEP:Q15] on $93.93B in revenues [PEP:Q3] implies a gross margin exceeding 54%—but FY2025 marked a notable step back, with operating income falling to $11.50B [PEP:Q11] from $12.89B in FY2024 [PEP:Q10] and net income declining to $8.24B [PEP:Q7] from $9.58B [PEP:Q6]. This reversal, against a backdrop of near-flat revenue growth, signals rising cost pressures or volume headwinds that are compressing PepsiCo's earnings power.

---

## Inventory Turnover

*Insufficient data*

---

PepsiCo, by contrast, generated $93.93B in FY2025 revenues [PEP:Q3] against a gross profit of $50.86B [PEP:Q15], reflecting a gross margin above 54%, but operating income fell to $11.50B [PEP:Q11] from a peak of $12.89B in FY2024 [PEP:Q10], and net income declined to $8.24B [PEP:Q7] from $9.58B the prior year [PEP:Q6]. In absolute dollar terms, PepsiCo still earns more net income than Costco on roughly one-third the revenue, underscoring its structurally superior margin profile—but the directional trends favor Costco, whose profits have grown every year in the window while PepsiCo's have reversed.
